headline: Scaling a Two-Million-Line Haskell Codebase at Mercury
lede: Maintaining high-volume financial systems requires more than just speed; it demands rigorous, type-safe boundaries. Mercury’s engineering team has turned pure functional programming into a pragmatic tool for managing institutional knowledge as they grow.

headline: Ladybird Browser Achieves New Performance Milestones in April
lede: The Ladybird project is moving beyond experimental status, integrating a fresh GTK4 frontend and refined memory management. By rebuilding the web stack from scratch in Rust, the team is proving that an independent engine can keep pace with modern performance demands.

headline: VideoLAN Launches Dav2d AV1 Decoder Initiative
lede: Efficiency in streaming now depends on royalty-free codecs. VideoLAN’s new high-performance AV1 decoder project aims to minimize bandwidth overhead and stabilize video playback across heterogeneous hardware platforms.

headline: Critical Soundness Vulnerability Found in Dusk's PLONK Library
lede: A fundamental flaw in zero-knowledge proof verification has exposed transaction systems to potential forgeries. Researchers are calling for immediate industry standardization of PLONK verification to prevent similar architectural collapses in privacy-preserving networks.

headline: Six Years of Iterating Maps on WatchOS
lede: Designing for the wrist forces a brutal distillation of UI. David Smith recounts the six-year evolution of his custom map engine, choosing bespoke topography over native tools to prioritize clarity in a constrained environment.

headline: Establishing a Universal 'Do Not Track' Environment Variable
lede: Telemetry has become a chaotic sprawl of tool-specific toggles. The do_not_track.sh initiative proposes a unified industry standard to restore user agency through a simple, environment-level configuration.

headline: Group Averages Often Obscure Individual Cognitive Pathways
lede: New research suggests that aggregate data in brain scans frequently masks how individuals uniquely regulate behavior. Focusing on real-time neural dynamics could transform the clinical approach to personalized psychiatry.

headline: Writing Structured YAML Specs to Tame AI Psychosis
lede: As reliance on autonomous coding agents grows, informal prompting has become a liability. Developers are shifting toward rigid, testable specifications to ensure consistency in AI-generated output.
pullquote: Rigorous, testable specifications are the essential bridge between human design and autonomous code.

headline: Neanderthals Operated Large-Scale Fat Factories 125,000 Years Ago
lede: Evidence from the Neumark-Nord site reveals that early populations mastered industrial-scale resource processing. This discovery challenges our perception of Neanderthals as simple scavengers, proving their capacity for long-term strategic survival planning.

headline: Microsoft Scales Back Aggressive Copilot Integration in Windows
lede: Following sustained user feedback regarding bloat and intrusive AI features, Microsoft is pivoting toward OS stability. This quality update marks a retreat from aggressive feature pushing in favor of core responsiveness and performance.
